name: prisma
description: Prisma is a modern DB toolkit to query, migrate and model your database

--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --datasource-provider
options.description: Define the datasource provider to use

options.name: --url
options.description: Define a custom datasource url

--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --data-proxy
options.description: Enable the Data Proxy in the Prisma Client

options.name: --no-hints
options.description: Hides the hint messages but still outputs errors and warnings

options.name: --no-engine
options.description: Generate a client for use with Accelerate only

options.name: --watch
options.description: Watch the Prisma schema and rerun after a change

options.name: --allow-no-models
options.description: Allow generating a client without models

--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

--port
options.description: Port to start Studio on

--browser
options.description: Browser to open Studio in

--hostname
options.description: Hostname to bind the Express server to

--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema

subcommands.name: dev
subcommands.description: The migrate dev command updates your database using migrations files during development

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --skip-seed
options.description: Skip triggering seed

options.name: --skip-generate
options.description: Skip triggering generators (e.g. Prisma Client)

options.name: --create-only
options.description: Create a new migration but do not apply it. The migration will be empty if there are no changes in Prisma schema

name: -n
--name
options.description: The name of the migration. If no name is provided, the CLI will prompt you

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset your database and apply all migrations, all data will be lost

--help

options.name: --skip-seed
options.description: Skip triggering seed

options.name: --skip-generate
options.description: Skip triggering generators (e.g. Prisma Client)

name: -f
--force
options.description: Skip the confirmation prompt

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Apply pending migrations to update the database schema in production/staging

--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.name: resolve
subcommands.description: Resolve issues with database migrations in deployment databases

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --applied
options.description: Record a specific migration as applied

options.name: --rolled-back
options.description: Record a specific migration as rolled back

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Compares the database schema from two arbitrary sources, and outputs the differences either as a human-readable summary (by default) or an executable script

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --from-url
options.description: A datasource url

options.name: --to-url
options.description: A datasource url

options.name: --from-empty
options.description: Flag to assume from is an empty datamodel

options.name: --to-empty
options.description: Flag to assume to is an empty datamodel

options.name: --from-schema-datamodel
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff

options.name: --to-schema-datamodel
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff

options.name: --from-schema-datasource
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff

options.name: --to-schema-datasource
options.description: Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff

options.name: --from-migrations
options.description: Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory

options.name: --to-migrations
options.description: Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory

options.name: --shadow-database-url
options.description: URL for the shadow database. Only required if using --from-migrations or --to-migrations

options.name: --script
options.description: Render a SQL script to stdout instead of the default human readable summary (not supported on MongoDB)

options.name: --exit-code
options.description: Change the exit code behavior to signal if the diff is empty or not (Empty: 0, Error: 1, Not empty: 2). Default behavior is Success: 0, Error: 1.`

subcommands.name: db
subcommands.description: Manage your database schema and lifecycle (Preview)

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --force
options.description: Ignore current Prisma schema file

options.name: --print
options.description: Print the introspected Prisma schema to stdout

options.name: --url
options.description: The datasource url

options.name: --composite-type-depth
options.description: Specify the depth for introspecting composite types (e.g. Embedded Documents in MongoDB). Number, default is -1 for infinite depth, 0 = off

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: This command pushes the state of your Prisma schema file to the database without using migrations files

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --skip-generate
options.description: Skip generation of artifacts such as Prisma Client

options.name: --force-reset
options.description: Resets the database and then updates the schema - useful if you need to start from scratch due to unexecutable migrations

options.name: --accept-data-loss
options.description: Ignore data loss warnings. This option is required if as a result of making the schema changes, data may be lost

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.name: execute
subcommands.description: Execute native commands to your database

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message

options.name: --schema
options.description: Custom path to your Prisma schema

options.name: --url
options.description: The datasource url

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to a file. The content will be sent as the script to be executed

options.name: --stdin
options.description: Use the terminal standard input as the script to be executed

name: version
-v
subcommands.description: Print current version of Prisma components
